zotero.abstractNote: Estimating the causal effect of multiple treatments is a fundamental problem in many fields, including medicine and policy-making. While existing methods for causal inference often focus on a single treatment, real-world scenarios frequently involve combinations of treatments. In this paper, we propose NCoRE, a novel framework for counterfactual representation learning that is specifically designed to handle combinations of treatments. NCoRE learns a shared representation that captures the underlying structure of the data while accounting for the dependencies between treatments. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on both synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that it outperforms existing methods in estimating the causal effects of treatment combinations.
